primary.title: BMI Calculator - Free Body Mass Index Calculator
primary.description: Calculate Body Mass Index (BMI) using metric or imperial measurements. Color-coded health status index scales and calculations.
primary.heading: BMI Calculator
primary.seo_content:
Our BMI Calculator computes body mass indices and indicates weight classifications. Here is the reference guide:

  • 1. BMI Formulas (Metric & Imperial)

    Metric Formula: BMI = weight (kg) / height² (m²)
    Imperial Formula: BMI = (weight (lbs) / height² (in²)) × 703
    Example: An adult weighing 70 kg at a height of 175 cm (1.75 m) has a BMI of 22.86.

  • 2. BMI Weight Classifications (WHO Chart)

    Underweight: BMI < 18.5 | Normal: 18.5 - 24.9 | Overweight: 25 - 29.9 | Obese: BMI ≥ 30.

examples.title: Standard metric assessment
examples.description: An adult weighing 70 kg at a height of 175 cm has a BMI of 22.86, placing them squarely in the healthy 'Normal weight' range.

examples.title: Imperial system conversion
examples.description: A person weighing 180 lbs at 5 feet 10 inches tall has a BMI of 25.82, which classifies as slightly 'Overweight'.

faqs.question: What is the standard BMI classification chart?
faqs.answer: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), the classifications are: Underweight (BMI < 18.5), Normal weight (18.5 to 24.9), Overweight (25 to 29.9), and Obese (BMI ≥ 30).

faqs.question: Is BMI a perfect indicator of body fat?
faqs.answer: No, BMI is a general correlation tool. It does not measure body fat directly and does not distinguish between muscle mass and fat mass, meaning athletes or bodybuilders might get a high BMI score despite low body fat.

ideal-weight-calculator.title: Ideal Weight Calculator - Find Your Healthy Weight Range
ideal-weight-calculator.description: Calculate your ideal body weight based on height, gender, and standard medical index formulas. Free client-side tool.
ideal-weight-calculator.heading: Ideal Weight Calculator
ideal-weight-calculator.seo_content:
Finding your ideal body weight assists in establishing clear, healthy lifestyle targets. Here is an example range calculation:

  • Healthy weight range

    Example: For a height of 170 cm (5 feet 7 inches), the healthy BMI-based weight range is between 53.5 kg (118 lbs) and 72 kg (159 lbs).

examples.title: Ideal weight range for a 170 cm adult
examples.description: For a height of 170 cm (5 feet 7 inches), the healthy BMI-based weight range is between 53.5 kg (118 lbs) and 72 kg (159 lbs).

examples.title: Devine formula estimate
examples.description: According to the traditional Devine formula, the ideal weight for a 6-foot-tall (183 cm) male is approximately 77.6 kg (171 lbs).

faqs.question: How is ideal weight calculated by medical formulas?
faqs.answer: Classic formulas (like Devine) use a baseline weight for 5 feet of height and add a fixed weight increment (e.g. 2.3 kg or 5 lbs) for every additional inch.

faqs.question: Why is the BMI range a better target than a single formula?
faqs.answer: The BMI range provides a flexible 18.5-24.9 gap, which accounts for differences in bone density, muscle distribution, and body frame sizes.

weight-loss-calculator.title: Weight Loss Calculator - Track Fitness Goals
weight-loss-calculator.description: Plan your fitness timeline. Estimate the weight drop needed to transition into a healthy weight category.
weight-loss-calculator.heading: Weight Loss Calculator
weight-loss-calculator.seo_content:
Estimate the weight drop needed to transition into a healthy weight category. Here is a standard weight reduction scenario:

  • Target Weight Reduction

    Example: A person weighing 85 kg at a height of 170 cm has a BMI of 29.4. To reach a normal BMI of 24.9, they need to lose 13 kg, bringing their weight down to 72 kg.

examples.title: Moving from Overweight to Normal
examples.description: A person weighing 85 kg at a height of 170 cm has a BMI of 29.4. To reach a normal BMI of 24.9, they need to lose 13 kg, bringing their weight down to 72 kg.

examples.title: Transitioning out of Obese category
examples.description: For a height of 5'8" and weight of 210 lbs (BMI 31.9), dropping 13 lbs is needed to exit the Obese range, and 46 lbs is needed to enter Normal weight.

faqs.question: What is a safe rate of weight loss?
faqs.answer: Medical professionals generally recommend losing weight at a slow, steady rate of 1 to 2 pounds (0.5 to 1 kg) per week through balanced diet adjustments and exercise.

faqs.question: How does target weight affect my daily caloric needs?
faqs.answer: As you lose weight, your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) decreases, meaning you will need slightly fewer calories to maintain or continue losing weight.
